---
title: "Standard Soil Laboratory Analysis"
---

Standard laboratory soil analysis provides the reference layer for many toolbox assessments. It is the most familiar route for measuring key chemical and physical variables in a structured way.

| Item | Description |
|---|---|
| **What it is** | A set of established laboratory analyses for core soil variables. |
| **Main purpose** | To provide reliable measurements for key physicochemical properties. |
| **Where used** | Laboratory |
| **Typical users** | Laboratories, researchers, advisors, land managers via service providers |
| **Typical outputs** | Lab values for soil texture, pH, EC, SOC, nutrients, CEC, and related properties |

## Main target variables

- pH,
- electrical conductivity,
- phosphorus,
- nitrogen,
- texture fractions,
- cation exchange capacity,
- soil organic carbon,
- and related variables depending on the analysis package.

## Descriptor groups supported

- salinity,
- nutrient-related variables,
- soil organic carbon,
- acidity,
- and part of the baseline descriptor set used for broader assessment.
